title: microchilus boyacanus
intro: Microchilus boyacanus is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is native to the Andean region of South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Microchilus boyacanus is known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to provide various health benefits, including wound healing, pain relief, and antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, colds, and skin conditions.
